attachment.content:
CAVEMAN MODE ACTIVE — level: full

Respond terse like smart caveman. All technical substance stay. Only fluff die.

## Persistence

ACTIVE EVERY RESPONSE. No revert after many turns. No filler drift. Still active if unsure. Off only: "stop caveman" / "normal mode".

Default: **full**. Switch: `/caveman lite|full|ultra`.

## Rules

Drop: articles (a/an/the), filler (just/really/basically/actually/simply), pleasantries (sure/certainly/of course/happy to), hedging. Fragments OK. Short synonyms (big not extensive, fix not "implement a solution for"). Technical terms exact. Code blocks unchanged. Errors quoted exact.

Pattern: `[thing] [action] [reason]. [next step].`

Not: "Sure! I'd be happy to help you with that. The issue you're experiencing is likely caused by..."
Yes: "Bug in auth middleware. Token expiry check use `<` not `<=`. Fix:"

## Intensity

| Level | What change |
|-------|------------|
| **full** | Drop articles, fragments OK, short synonyms. Classic caveman |

Example — "Why React component re-render?"
- full: "New object ref each render. Inline object prop = new ref = re-render. Wrap in `useMemo`."

Example — "Explain database connection pooling."
- full: "Pool reuse open DB connections. No new connection per request. Skip handshake overhead."

## Auto-Clarity

Drop caveman for: security warnings, irreversible action confirmations, multi-step sequences where fragment order risks misread, user asks to clarify or repeats question. Resume caveman after clear part done.

Example — destructive op:
> **Warning:** This will permanently delete all rows in the `users` table and cannot be undone.
> ```sql
> DROP TABLE users;
> ```
> Caveman resume. Verify backup exist first.

## Boundaries

Code/commits/PRs: write normal. "stop caveman" or "normal mode": revert. Level persist until changed or session end.

STATUSLINE SETUP NEEDED: The caveman plugin includes a statusline badge showing active mode (e.g. [CAVEMAN], [CAVEMAN:ULTRA]). It is not configured yet. To enable, add this to C:\Users\manaz\.claude\settings.json: "statusLine": { "type": "command", "command": "powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File \"C:\\Users\\manaz\\.claude\\plugins\\cache\\caveman\\caveman\\84cc3c14fa1e\\hooks\\caveman-statusline.ps1\"" } Proactively offer to set this up for the user on first interaction.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- compress: /compress — Capture Session to Searchable Log
- preserve: /preserve — Save Session Knowledge to CLAUDE.md
- resume: /resume — Restore Session Context
- caveman:caveman: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by speaking like caveman while keeping full technical accuracy. Supports intensity levels: lite, full (default), ultra, wenyan-lite, wenyan-full, wenyan-ultra. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman. Also auto-triggers when token efficiency is requested.
- caveman:caveman-commit: Ultra-compressed commit message generator. Cuts noise from commit messages while preserving intent and reasoning. Conventional Commits format. Subject ≤50 chars, body only when "why" isn't obvious. Use when user says "write a commit", "commit message", "generate commit", "/commit", or invokes /caveman-commit. Auto-triggers when staging changes.
- caveman:caveman-help: Quick-reference card for all caveman modes, skills, and commands. One-shot display, not a persistent mode. Trigger: /caveman-help, "caveman help", "what caveman commands", "how do I use caveman".
- caveman:caveman-review: Ultra-compressed code review comments. Cuts noise from PR feedback while preserving the actionable signal. Each comment is one line: location, problem, fix. Use when user says "review this PR", "code review", "review the diff", "/review", or invokes /caveman-review. Auto-triggers when reviewing pull requests.
- caveman:compress: Compress natural language memory files (CLAUDE.md, todos, preferences) into caveman format to save input tokens. Preserves all technical substance, code, URLs, and structure. Compressed version overwrites the original file. Human-readable backup saved as FILE.original.md. Trigger: /caveman:compress <filepath> or "compress memory file"
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content: Launching skill: resume

content.text:
# /resume — Restore Session Context

Load project memory and recent session logs to orient quickly in a new conversation.

## Usage
```
/resume              → Last 3 sessions + CLAUDE.md
/resume 5            → Last 5 sessions + CLAUDE.md
/resume auth         → Last 3 + search for "auth" mentions
/resume 10 jira      → Last 10 + search for "jira" mentions
```

Parse arguments:
- If first arg is a number → session count
- If first arg is text (or second arg) → topic search keyword
- Defaults: count=3, no topic search

## Steps

**1. Find project root**
Walk up from current directory until finding CLAUDE.md or .git. This is the project root.

**2. Load CLAUDE.md**
Read the full CLAUDE.md. Extract:
- Current project phase / status
- Active blockers
- Key decisions and patterns
- Next steps

**3. Locate session logs**
Look for `{project-root}/CC-Session-Logs/` directory.

**4. Load recent session summaries**
- List all `.md` files in CC-Session-Logs/, sorted newest-first by filename (timestamp prefix)
- For each of the N most recent logs: read ONLY the content BEFORE the `## Raw Session Log` marker
- NEVER load the raw log section — this keeps token usage constant regardless of archive size

**5. Topic search (if keyword provided)**
Scale behavior based on log count:
- Under 100 logs: scan all summaries already loaded for keyword mentions
- 100+ logs: use grep on CC-Session-Logs/ to find files where keyword appears in summary section; read top 5 matches

**6. Output structured report**

```
## Project Context
Phase: {current phase}
Blockers: {active blockers or "none"}
Key patterns: {1-3 lines}

## Most Recent Session — {date} — {topic}
Keywords: {confidence keywords}
Decisions: {key decisions}
Outcome: {what was accomplished}
Left off: {resume context}

## Previous Sessions ({count-1} of {N} shown)
- {date} — {topic}: {one-line summary}
- {date} — {topic}: {one-line summary}
...

## Related Sessions (topic: "{keyword}")  ← only if search requested
- {date} — {topic}: {one-line summary}
...

## Recommended Next Steps
{2-3 concrete next actions based on loaded context}
```

## Edge Cases
- No CLAUDE.md → "No CLAUDE.md found. Run /preserve after setup, or create one now?"
- No session logs → Output CLAUDE.md context only, note no logs exist yet
- Fewer logs than requested → Return all available, note the shortfall
- Empty CC-Session-Logs/ → Note it, continue with CLAUDE.md only

## Key Principle
Token efficiency above all: never load raw logs. Summary headers only. The "## Raw Session Log" marker is a hard boundary — stop reading at that line.
Let me check the jobfilter folder for session logs and CLAUDE.md.

content.content:
1	# JobFilter — Claude Rules
2	
3	## Core
4	Think first. Read before editing. No blind changes.
5	
6	## Efficiency
7	- Read → then edit
8	- Target specific lines/functions only
9	- No repeated file reads
10	- Avoid full-file reads unless needed
11	- Keep tokens low, outputs short
12	
13	## Editing
14	- Make small, precise changes
15	- Don't rewrite entire files
16	- Keep code simple and clean
17	
18	## Debugging
19	- Locate issue → read relevant code → fix minimally
20	- No trial-and-error loops
21	
22	## Project Goal
23	- High-quality job leads (with contact info where possible)
24	- Filter junk/duplicates
25	- Clean, simple outputs
26	- Subtle push to paid features
27	
28	## Data Rules
29	- Free sources only
30	- Prefer real, usable leads (not scraped junk)
31	- Validate before output
32	
33	## Build Style
34	- Simple > complex
35	- Modular
36	- Fast to implement
37	
38	## Avoid
39	- Re-reading files
40	- Large outputs
41	- Overengineering
42	- Wasting tokens
43	
44	## Execution
45	Precise. Fast. Surgical.
46

file.content:
# JobFilter — Claude Rules

## Core
Think first. Read before editing. No blind changes.

## Efficiency
- Read → then edit
- Target specific lines/functions only
- No repeated file reads
- Avoid full-file reads unless needed
- Keep tokens low, outputs short

## Editing
- Make small, precise changes
- Don't rewrite entire files
- Keep code simple and clean

## Debugging
- Locate issue → read relevant code → fix minimally
- No trial-and-error loops

## Project Goal
- High-quality job leads (with contact info where possible)
- Filter junk/duplicates
- Clean, simple outputs
- Subtle push to paid features

## Data Rules
- Free sources only
- Prefer real, usable leads (not scraped junk)
- Validate before output

## Build Style
- Simple > complex
- Modular
- Fast to implement

## Avoid
- Re-reading files
- Large outputs
- Overengineering
- Wasting tokens

## Execution
Precise. Fast. Surgical.

content.content:
1	# PROJECT_STATUS — JobFilter
2	
3	## What Was Fixed
4	
5	### API Reliability
6	- `/api/leads/scan` now ALWAYS returns JSON — never HTML
7	- `try/catch` wraps entire route handler
8	- Error catch block returns `{ error, leads: SCAN_FALLBACK, ... }` with 7 real fallback jobs
9	- Server-side `SCAN_FALLBACK` constant injects when live scan returns 0 results
10	
11	### Critical Bug: Region Mismatch
12	- `postcodes.io` returns broad ONS region names ("North West", "London")
13	- `DirectorySignal` keyed on specific names ("Greater Manchester", "East London")
14	- When postcodes.io was UP, directory returned 0 leads — fallback always triggered
15	- **Fixed**: `lookupPostcode` now prefers local outward-code map when it returns a specific region
16	
17	### Demo Page
18	- Loading state, error state, fallback state all implemented
19	- Input: postcode + trade selector
20	- Button: "Run Live Scan"
21	- Cards: title, location, value, urgency, source
22	- On any API failure → shows sample leads (never blank)
23	- Frontend validates `Content-Type: application/json` before parsing
24	
25	### Navbar
26	- Updated: HOME / DEMO / CODEX / VANTAGE / VICINITY / PRICING
27	- All routes exist and render pages
28	
29	### Visual Style
30	- Header: black background, yellow border, yellow UK badge
31	- Nav: white links, yellow hover, yellow GET STARTED button
32	- Pages: yellow (#facc15) + black dominant, thick borders, boxy, bold
33	
34	### New Data Source: Companies House
35	- File: `leadEngine/fetchers/companiesHouseFetcher.ts`
36	- Implements strategic arbitrage signals from the Intelligence Report:
37	  - Signal 1: Recently incorporated companies (0–12 months) in growth SIC codes
38	  - Signal 2: Growth sector SIC pivots → premises fit-out signals
39	  - Signal 3: Construction/property/hospitality/tech companies → direct trade need
40	- 25 SIC code mappings → trade type + value estimate + urgency
41	- Wired into `scan.ts` pipeline alongside ContractsFinder, FTS, PlanningData
42	
43	### Planning Data Fetcher
44	- Removed broken `geometry_relation: within` (requires polygon, not a point)
45	- Fixed to use point-based lat/lon lookup
46	- Improved field mapping to handle `json_data` nested structure
47	
48	### Config
49	- `freeTierLimit` raised from 3 → 5 (shows more on demo without subscription)
50	- `SOURCE_CH` env flag added for Companies House toggle
51	
52	## What Works Now
53	
54	| Feature | Status |
55	|---------|--------|
56	| `npm run dev` | ✓ Starts on localhost:3000 |
57	| `/api/leads/scan` returns JSON | ✓ Always |
58	| Demo scan button | ✓ Works, shows results |
59	| Fallback leads on failure | ✓ 7 jobs shown |
60	| DirectorySignal data | ✓ Fixed region matching |
61	| ContractsFinder | ✓ Live (public API, no key) |
62	| FTS (Find a Tender) | ✓ Live (public API, no key) |
63	| PlanningData | ✓ Live (public API, no key) |
64	| Companies House signals | ✓ With API key |
65	| All 6 nav routes | ✓ Working |
66	| Build (`npm run build`) | ✓ Clean |
67	| TypeScript (`tsc --noEmit`) | ✓ Zero errors |
68	
69	## Remaining Risks
70	
71	1. **ContractsFinder/FTS latency**: These APIs can take 5–10 seconds per call. The 9s timeout in config may cause them to fail silently on slow connections.
72	
73	2. **Planning Data API schema**: `planning.data.gov.uk` has changed their API schema multiple times. If they change field names again, the fetcher returns 0 planning leads (fallback covers this).
74	
75	3. **Companies House** requires API key for live data. Without key, this source is silently skipped.
76	
77	4. **No auth on `/api/leads/scan`**: Anyone can call it. Rate limiting is not implemented.
78	
79	## Manual Steps Required
80	
81	### To enable Companies House signals (free):
82	1. Register at https://developer.company-information.service.gov.uk/get-started
83	2. Create a live application, get API key
84	3. Add to `.env`:
85	```
86	COMPANIES_HOUSE_API_KEY=your_key_here
87	```
88	
89	### To configure Stripe (payments):
90	```
91	STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_live_...
92	APP_URL=https://yourdomain.com
93	```
94	
95	### To run:
96	```bash
97	npm run dev        # development (localhost:3000)
98	npm run build      # production build
99	```
100	
101	### Environment file:
102	Create `.env` in project root with required keys (see above).
103	
104	## Architecture Summary
105	
106	```
107	POST /api/leads/scan
108	  └── scan(postcode, trade, tier)
109	        ├── ContractsFinder (public, no key)
110	        ├── FTS / Find a Tender (public, no key)
111	        ├── PlanningData (public, no key)
112	        ├── CompaniesHouse (free key required)
113	        └── DirectorySignal (internal, always works)
114	```
115	
116	All sources run in parallel. Any failure → that source returns empty, others continue.
117	If all sources return 0 leads → server injects SCAN_FALLBACK (7 real UK trade jobs).
118	Frontend ALSO has FALLBACK_LEADS as secondary safety net.
119

file.content:
# PROJECT_STATUS — JobFilter

## What Was Fixed

### API Reliability
- `/api/leads/scan` now ALWAYS returns JSON — never HTML
- `try/catch` wraps entire route handler
- Error catch block returns `{ error, leads: SCAN_FALLBACK, ... }` with 7 real fallback jobs
- Server-side `SCAN_FALLBACK` constant injects when live scan returns 0 results

### Critical Bug: Region Mismatch
- `postcodes.io` returns broad ONS region names ("North West", "London")
- `DirectorySignal` keyed on specific names ("Greater Manchester", "East London")
- When postcodes.io was UP, directory returned 0 leads — fallback always triggered
- **Fixed**: `lookupPostcode` now prefers local outward-code map when it returns a specific region

### Demo Page
- Loading state, error state, fallback state all implemented
- Input: postcode + trade selector
- Button: "Run Live Scan"
- Cards: title, location, value, urgency, source
- On any API failure → shows sample leads (never blank)
- Frontend validates `Content-Type: application/json` before parsing

### Navbar
- Updated: HOME / DEMO / CODEX / VANTAGE / VICINITY / PRICING
- All routes exist and render pages

### Visual Style
- Header: black background, yellow border, yellow UK badge
- Nav: white links, yellow hover, yellow GET STARTED button
- Pages: yellow (#facc15) + black dominant, thick borders, boxy, bold

### New Data Source: Companies House
- File: `leadEngine/fetchers/companiesHouseFetcher.ts`
- Implements strategic arbitrage signals from the Intelligence Report:
  - Signal 1: Recently incorporated companies (0–12 months) in growth SIC codes
  - Signal 2: Growth sector SIC pivots → premises fit-out signals
  - Signal 3: Construction/property/hospitality/tech companies → direct trade need
- 25 SIC code mappings → trade type + value estimate + urgency
- Wired into `scan.ts` pipeline alongside ContractsFinder, FTS, PlanningData

### Planning Data Fetcher
- Removed broken `geometry_relation: within` (requires polygon, not a point)
- Fixed to use point-based lat/lon lookup
- Improved field mapping to handle `json_data` nested structure

### Config
- `freeTierLimit` raised from 3 → 5 (shows more on demo without subscription)
- `SOURCE_CH` env flag added for Companies House toggle

## What Works Now

| Feature | Status |
|---------|--------|
| `npm run dev` | ✓ Starts on localhost:3000 |
| `/api/leads/scan` returns JSON | ✓ Always |
| Demo scan button | ✓ Works, shows results |
| Fallback leads on failure | ✓ 7 jobs shown |
| DirectorySignal data | ✓ Fixed region matching |
| ContractsFinder | ✓ Live (public API, no key) |
| FTS (Find a Tender) | ✓ Live (public API, no key) |
| PlanningData | ✓ Live (public API, no key) |
| Companies House signals | ✓ With API key |
| All 6 nav routes | ✓ Working |
| Build (`npm run build`) | ✓ Clean |
| TypeScript (`tsc --noEmit`) | ✓ Zero errors |

## Remaining Risks

1. **ContractsFinder/FTS latency**: These APIs can take 5–10 seconds per call. The 9s timeout in config may cause them to fail silently on slow connections.

2. **Planning Data API schema**: `planning.data.gov.uk` has changed their API schema multiple times. If they change field names again, the fetcher returns 0 planning leads (fallback covers this).

3. **Companies House** requires API key for live data. Without key, this source is silently skipped.

4. **No auth on `/api/leads/scan`**: Anyone can call it. Rate limiting is not implemented.

## Manual Steps Required

### To enable Companies House signals (free):
1. Register at https://developer.company-information.service.gov.uk/get-started
2. Create a live application, get API key
3. Add to `.env`:
```
COMPANIES_HOUSE_API_KEY=your_key_here
```

### To configure Stripe (payments):
```
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_live_...
APP_URL=https://yourdomain.com
```

### To run:
```bash
npm run dev        # development (localhost:3000)
npm run build      # production build
```

### Environment file:
Create `.env` in project root with required keys (see above).

## Architecture Summary

```
POST /api/leads/scan
  └── scan(postcode, trade, tier)
        ├── ContractsFinder (public, no key)
        ├── FTS / Find a Tender (public, no key)
        ├── PlanningData (public, no key)
        ├── CompaniesHouse (free key required)
        └── DirectorySignal (internal, always works)
```

All sources run in parallel. Any failure → that source returns empty, others continue.
If all sources return 0 leads → server injects SCAN_FALLBACK (7 real UK trade jobs).
Frontend ALSO has FALLBACK_LEADS as secondary safety net.

file.content:
# Coordinator Analysis — 2026-04-25

## Status
First run. No codex-output folder exists. No product_context.txt found. Baseline read of App.tsx.

## Critical Issues Found

### 1. UNRESOLVED GIT MERGE CONFLICTS (BLOCKER)
App.tsx is littered with unresolved conflict markers:
- `<<<<<<< ours` / `>>>>>>> theirs` appear 8+ times throughout the JSX
- File will not compile. App is completely broken.
- Must be resolved before any other work matters.

### 2. DUPLICATE FUNCTION DECLARATIONS (BLOCKER)
Inside the App component, these are declared 8+ times each:
- `resolveRegionFromPostcode`
- `toDisplayUrgency`
This is a TypeScript error — const redeclaration in same scope.

### Root Cause
Codex ran multiple merge passes without resolving conflicts first.
Each pass appended instead of replaced.

## Structure (what works when unblocked)

- Intake Engine: postcode + trade → scan → 3 free leads → paywall
- Free Tools: 10 calculators (quote, dayrate, lead value, markup, etc.)
- Waitlist modal → Firebase + /api/waitlist
- ROI calculator (quotesPerWeek, milesDriven → annual savings)
- Analytics via dataLayer

## What Codex Should Fix Next

Priority order:
1. Resolve all merge conflicts (pick "ours" or "theirs" — keep the more complete version)
2. Remove duplicate function declarations (keep one copy of each)
3. Verify file compiles (no TypeScript errors)
4. Then: check nav links, hero CTA, waitlist flow all intact

## Patterns to Watch
- Codex merge passes are creating duplicate code blocks
- Future runs must diff before writing, not append

# Next Codex Task — 2026-04-25

## PRIORITY: CRITICAL — App does not compile

### Task: Fix App.tsx merge conflicts and duplicate declarations

**File:** `src/App.tsx`

**Problems to fix (in order):**

1. **Resolve all git merge conflict markers**
   - Search for `<<<<<<< ours`, `=======`, `>>>>>>> theirs`
   - For each conflict block: keep the "ours" version (the more complete/recent one)
   - Delete all conflict markers

2. **Remove duplicate function declarations**
   - `resolveRegionFromPostcode` is declared 8+ times — keep ONE copy (lines ~250–255 region)
   - `toDisplayUrgency` is declared 8+ times — keep ONE copy
   - Both belong inside the App component, after state declarations, before `trackEvent`

3. **Verify after fix:**
   - No `const` redeclarations in same scope
   - No conflict markers remain
   - JSX is valid (no unclosed tags from conflict removal)

**Do NOT:**
- Change any logic or UI
- Rename any variables
- Restructure the component

**Output:** Confirm the file compiles clean. List what was removed.

---
name: JobFilter Product Definitions
description: Core product structure — intake engine + 3 advantages + free tools. NEVER reinterpret. Must inform all site decisions.
type: project
originSessionId: 4ddb9185-24d0-4d3f-8d11-dd59a954c7b8
---
Everything on site must push toward subscribing to INTAKE ENGINE.

**Why:** This is the paid core. All other products support winning jobs, not replace the engine.
**How to apply:** Structure all pages/sections around intake engine CTA. Advantages support it. Free tools attract/trust-build.

---

## MAIN PRODUCT: INTAKE ENGINE (subscription)

- Finds jobs, filters them, sends only high-quality opportunities
- What users pay for
- Every site decision must push toward → subscribing

---

## ADVANTAGES (supporting, NOT random features):

### VANTAGE
Tagline: "Stop losing £1M bids to prettier firms"
- Transforms tender docs → bid decks, 3D renders, infographics
- Purpose: make smaller firms look tier-1, win bigger jobs

### VICINITY
Tagline: "Stop letting your best work rot in your camera roll"
- Takes completed jobs → visual marketing assets, WhatsApp-ready, website-ready content
- Purpose: turn past work into new work automatically
- Acts like: one-tap marketing agency for tradesmen

### CODEX
Tagline: "Turn complex technical content into high-conversion sales assets"
- Input: instruction manuals, engineering schematics
- Output: how-it-works videos, sales carousels, competitor battle cards
- Purpose: help tradesmen and technical firms sell better, win jobs

---

## FREE TOOLS
- Always free, no catch
- Purpose: attract users, build trust
- Position: "Use our free tools forever — no catch"

---

## WHAT JOBFILTER IS (never forget):
"A system that gets tradesmen better jobs and helps them win them"

## WHAT JOBFILTER IS NOT:
- NOT a tool directory
- NOT a feature list
- NOT a SaaS dashboard

---

## Project Context

**Phase:** App broken — merge conflict blocker  
**Blockers:** `src/App.tsx` won't compile

---

## Last Session — 2026-04-25

**What happened:**
- Coordinator ran, found `src/App.tsx` completely broken
- 8+ unresolved git merge conflict markers (`<<<<<<< ours` / `>>>>>>> theirs`)
- `resolveRegionFromPostcode` + `toDisplayUrgency` each declared 8+ times → TypeScript error
- Root cause: Codex ran multiple merge passes, appended instead of resolved

**What was accomplished before session:**
- All API fetchers working (ContractsFinder, FTS, PlanningData, CompaniesHouse, DirectorySignal)
- Region mismatch bug fixed
- Demo page, navbar, fallback leads all solid
- Build + TypeScript clean

**Left off:** `next-codex-task.md` queued — fix App.tsx merge conflicts + duplicate declarations

---

## Recommended Next Steps

1. **Fix `src/App.tsx`** — resolve conflict markers (keep "ours"), remove 7 of 8 duplicate function declarations
2. **Verify compile** — `tsc --noEmit` must pass clean
3. **Then** check nav links, hero CTA, waitlist flow still intact

Want me to fix `App.tsx` now?
